questions.text: Describe your organization’s business background and ownership structure, including all parent and subsidiary relationships.
Have you had an unplanned disruption to this product/service in the past 12 months?
Do you have a dedicated Information Security staff or office?
Do you have a dedicated Software and System Development team(s)? (e.g., Customer Support, Implementation, Product Management, etc.)
Does your product process protected health information (PHI) or any data covered by the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act?
Will data regulated by PCI DSS reside in the vended product?
Use this area to share information about your environment that will assist those who are assessing your company data security program.
Have you undergone a SSAE 18 / SOC 2 audit?
Have you completed the Cloud Security Alliance (CSA) CAIQ?
Have you received the Cloud Security Alliance STAR certification?
Do you conform with a specific industry standard security framework? (e.g., NIST Cybersecurity Framework, CIS Controls, ISO 27001, etc.)
Can the systems that hold the institution's data be compliant with NIST SP 800-171 and/or CMMC Level 2 standards?
Can you provide overall system and/or application architecture diagrams including a full description of the data flow for all components of the system?
Does your organization have a data privacy policy?
Do you have a documented, and currently implemented, employee onboarding and offboarding policy?
Do you have a well-documented Business Continuity Plan (BCP) that is tested annually?
Do you have a well-documented Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP) that is tested annually?
Do you have a documented change management process?
Are access controls for institutional accounts based on structured rules, such as role-based access control (RBAC), attribute-based access control (ABAC), or policy-based access control (PBAC)?
Are access controls for staff within your organization based on structured rules, such as RBAC, ABAC, or PBAC?
Do you have a documented and currently implemented strategy for securing employee workstations when they work remotely (i.e., not in a trusted computing environment)?
Does the system provide data input validation and error messages?
Are you using a web application firewall (WAF)?
Do you have a process and implemented procedures for managing your software supply chain (e.g., libraries, repositories, frameworks, etc.)?
Does your solution support single sign-on (SSO) protocols for user and administrator authentication?
Does your organization participate in InCommon or another eduGAIN-affiliated trust federation?
Does your application support integration with other authentication and authorization systems?
Does your solution support any of the following Web SSO standards? [e.g., SAML2 (with redirect flow), OIDC, CAS, or other]
Do you support differentiation between email address and user identifier?
Do you have a systems management and configuration strategy that encompasses servers, appliances, cloud services, applications, and mobile devices (company and employee owned)?
Will the institution be notified of major changes to your environment that could impact the institution's security posture?
Are your systems and applications scanned for vulnerabilities [that are then remediated] prior to new releases?
Have your systems and applications had a third-party security assessment completed in the past year?
Do you have policy and procedure, currently implemented, guiding how security risks are mitigated until patches can be applied?

Does the environment provide for dedicated single-tenant capabilities? If not, describe how your product or environment separates data from different customers (e.g., logically, physically, single tenancy, multi-tenancy).
Is sensitive data encrypted, using secure protocols/algorithms, in transport? (e.g., system-to-client)
Is sensitive data encrypted, using secure protocols/algorithms, in storage? (e.g., disk encryption, at-rest, files, and within a running database)
Are involatile backup copies made according to predefined schedules and securely stored and protected?
Can the institution extract a full or partial backup of data?

Do you have a media handling process that is documented and currently implemented that meets established business needs and regulatory requirements, including end-of-life, repurposing, and data sanitization procedures?
Does your staff (or third party) have access to institutional data (e.g., financial, PHI or other sensitive information) within the application/system?
Does your company manage the physical data center where the institution's data will reside?
Are you generally able to accomodate storing each institution's data within their geographic region?
Does the hosting provider have a SOC 2 Type 2 report available?
Does your organization have physical security controls and policies in place?
Do you have physical access control and video surveillance to prevent/detect unauthorized access to your data center?
Do you enforce network segmentation between trusted and untrusted networks (i.e., Internet, DMZ, Extranet, etc.)?
Are you utilizing a stateful packet inspection (SPI) firewall?
Do you use an automated IDS/IPS system to monitor for intrusions?
Are you employing any next-generation persistent threat (NGPT) monitoring?
Do you require connectivity to the institution's network for support/administration or access into any existing systems for integration purposes?
Do you have a formal incident response plan?
Do you have an incident response process and reporting in place to investigate any potential incidents and report actual incidents?
Do you carry cyber-risk insurance to protect against unforeseen service outages, data that is lost or stolen, and security incidents?
Do you have either an internal incident response team or retain an external team?
Do you have the capability to respond to incidents on a 24 x 7 x 365 basis?
Can you share the organization chart, mission statement, and policies for your information security unit?
Are information security principles designed into the product lifecycle?
Do you have a documented information security policy?
Will institutional data be shared with or hosted by any third parties? (e.g., any entity not wholly owned by your company is considered a third party)
Do you perform security assessments of third-party companies with which you share data? (e.g., hosting providers, cloud services, PaaS, IaaS, SaaS)
Do you have an implemented third-party management strategy?
Do you have a process and implemented procedures for managing your hardware supply chain? (e.g., telecommunications equipment, export licensing, computing devices)
